## Results

The latent change-score model reports within-child longitudinal couplings. These
are conditional predictive associations among latent trajectories, not causal
effects.

In plain terms: a coupling coefficient says how much the level of one skill at one
wave predicts the later change in another skill, holding the rest of the model
fixed.

```{python}
# | echo: false
coupling_summary = _csv("coupling_summary.csv")
coupling_summary if coupling_summary is not None else "No coupling summary."
```

_How to read the coupling table:_ each row is one directed coupling, written
`from -> to` (the level of `from` predicting the later change in `to`). `mean` is
the posterior average of that coupling; `lo` and `hi` are the ends of the 89%
credible interval, so a range that stays on one side of 0 is a coupling the data
support in that direction. `prob_pos` is the posterior probability the coupling is
positive (near 1 = confidently positive, near 0 = confidently negative, near 0.5 =
undecided).

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if coupling_summary is not None:
    _g = coupling_summary[
        coupling_summary["coefficient"].str.contains("->", regex=False)
    ]
    if len(_g):
        _r = _g.iloc[0]
        print(
            f"Headline coupling `{_r.coefficient}`: mean {_r['mean']:+.3f} "
            f"(50% CrI {_r.lo50:+.3f} to {_r.hi50:+.3f}; "
            f"89% {_r.lo:+.3f} to {_r.hi:+.3f}); "
            f"P(> 0) = {_r.prob_pos:.3f}. Read agreement with LRP65 and the "
            "mechanism models rather than treating this one coefficient as decisive."
        )
```

::: {.callout-note title="Model-check emphasis"}

Because this model has latent trajectories, process noise and only four waves,
sampling diagnostics and posterior predictive checks are part of the result. A
wide interval or fragile sampler is an honest finding, not just a nuisance.

:::


```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Crossover-aware shapes (LCSM-081/082) also write the window-1 randomised arm
# contrast; absent for the pooled-intercept LRP67, so this renders nothing there.
_itt = _csv("itt_window1_contrast.csv")
if _itt is not None:
    print("### Window-1 randomised contrast (ITT consistency check)\n")
    print(
        "The arm x window change intercepts make the window-1 cell contrast "
        "(immediate - waitlist) a **randomised** latent ITT contrast on the "
        "logit change scale. It is reported as a consistency check against the "
        "ITT suite (direction and rough magnitude), not as a new headline "
        "effect.\n"
    )
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_itt if _itt is not None else None
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Reciprocal-dominance shapes (LCSM-082) also write the SD-standardised
# contrast; absent elsewhere, so this renders nothing there.
_dom = _csv("dominance_summary.csv")
if _dom is not None:
    print("### Reciprocal dominance (SD-standardised couplings)\n")
    print(
        "Each direction's coupling is standardised per posterior draw by the "
        "model's own latent scales (`g* = g * sd(prior source levels) / "
        "sd(target changes)`), so the two directions are comparable; the "
        "dominance row is the posterior of the absolute-value difference. Both "
        "directions are broadly-confounded adjusted associations - read the "
        "contrast as hypothesis-generating only.\n"
    )
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
_dom if _dom is not None else None
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if _has("child_fit_panels.png"):
    print("### Per-child fit\n")
    print(_img("child_fit_panels.png", "Per-child fitted-vs-observed small multiples for the focal reading measure (observed dots, posterior-predictive band)"))
    print(
        "\n_How to read it:_ each panel is one child (indexed, never identified). Red dots are that "
        "child's observed scores on the focal reading measure (named in the figure title) across the "
        "four waves; the green band is the model's posterior-predictive 89% interval **for that same "
        "child**, built from the child's fitted latent trajectory (a *same-children* display). To keep "
        "one figure, only the focal outcome is drawn; the other measures behave analogously. Panels "
        "flagged with a high Pareto-k are the worst-fitting children (aggregated across all measures), "
        "shown on purpose; the rest are a seeded random draw."
    )
```
